title: Lepiactis virgata
taxonomy: Lepiactis virgata is classified within the family Asparagaceae. This classification places it within the order Asparagales. Lepiactis virgata is further grouped within the genus Lepiactis, which is commonly known as the sea squill or sea lily. The genus Lepiactis is characterized by its unique, bulbous base and long, strap-like leaves. Lepiactis virgata's classification highlights its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asparagaceae family.

intro: Lepiactis virgata, also known as Sea Squill or White Squill, is a type of bulbous perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its white flowers and edible bulbs. The bulbs of Lepiactis virgata have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as being used to treat respiratory issues and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Lepiactis virgata include alkaloids, saponins, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the bulbs of Lepiactis virgata are used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and dried extracts, often used in the treatment of respiratory conditions, including bronchitis and asthma.
